BillingAccount.description:
Billing Account represents the payment entity that owns one or more organizations.
Tier limits and quotas are enforced at the billing account level, not per-organization.

This prevents abuse where users could create multiple orgs to multiply their quotas.
For example:
- Business tier ($29) = 10k links/month across ALL orgs in the billing account
- Not 10k per org (which would be 30k for 3 orgs)

provider_customer_id.description: Polar customer ID for the billing account

raw_token.description: The raw token - show this ONLY ONCE to the user

redirect_type.description:
HTTP redirect type: 301 (permanent) or 307 (temporary).
Default is 301, available on Pro+ plans.

forward_query_params.description:
Whether to forward visitor query params to the destination (Pro+ only).
None = use org default (resolved at KV write time).

redirect_type.description:
HTTP redirect type: 301 (permanent) or 307 (temporary).
Default is 301 for SEO, 307 available on Pro+ plans.

oneOf.description: UTM parameters baked into this link (Pro+ only).

analytics_gated.description: Whether analytics data is gated due to tier limits
Whether analytics data is gated due to tier limits

OrgWithRole.description: An organization with the current user's membership role attached

Organization.description:
Organization (team/tenant) model.

Tier limits are enforced at the billing account level via `billing_account.tier`.
The organization is linked to a billing account which contains the authoritative tier.

billing_account_id.description:
Link to the billing account that owns this organization.
Tier limits are enforced at the billing account level.

PaginationMeta.description: Pagination metadata for paginated API responses

has_next.description: Whether there is a next page

has_prev.description: Whether there is a previous page

total.description: Total number of items across all pages

TimeRange.description:
Time range specification for analytics queries
Extensible design that can grow to support custom ranges, timezones, comparisons, etc.

oneOf.description: Custom date range with specific timestamps

redirect_type.description:
HTTP redirect type: 301 (permanent) or 307 (temporary).
Available on Pro+ plans.

UtmParams.description:
Standard Google UTM parameters attached to a link.
All fields are optional; only non-empty values are appended to the destination URL.

info.description:
API documentation for the Rushomon URL shortener service.

## Authentication

Most endpoints require authentication using either:
- **Session cookies** (for web application users)
- **Bearer tokens** (for API keys)

### Using API Keys

1. Create an API key in the dashboard (Pro tier or higher required)
2. Include the key in the Authorization header:
   ```
   Authorization: Bearer ro_pat_...
   ```
## Response Format

All responses are JSON formatted. Error responses follow this structure:
```json
{
  "error": "Error message describing what went wrong"
}
```

## Pagination

List endpoints support pagination with `page` and `limit` query parameters:
- `page`: Page number (1-indexed, default: 1)
- `limit`: Items per page (1-100, default: 20)
info.title: Rushomon URL Shortener API

parameters.description: Search by email or org name
parameters.name: search

post.summary: Reset billing account to free tier

post.description: Manually triggers the expired-subscription downgrade cron job. Downgrades all billing accounts whose subscriptions have expired to the Free tier

post.summary: Sync and cache products from Polar

parameters.description: Filter by status: pending, reviewed, dismissed
parameters.name: status

400.description: Cannot delete self or last org admin
Invalid role or cannot demote last admin
Cannot suspend self or last admin

get.description: Returns aggregate click analytics for the entire organization. Includes total clicks, unique links clicked, clicks over time, top links, referrers, countries, and user agents. The time range is capped by tier retention (7 days Free, 365 days Pro, unlimited Business/Unlimited)

parameters.description: Number of days to look back (default: 7)
parameters.name: days

parameters.description: Unix timestamp range start (alternative to days)
parameters.name: start

200.description: Org analytics response with clicks, top links, referrers, countries, and user agents

get.description: Handles the OAuth provider callback. Validates the state parameter, exchanges the authorization code for tokens, creates or updates the user, issues a session cookie, and redirects to the dashboard

parameters.description: Authorization code from the OAuth provider
parameters.name: code

302.description: Redirect to dashboard on success or home on failure

get.description: Redirects the user to GitHub to begin the OAuth 2.0 authorization flow
Redirects the user to Google to begin the OAuth 2.0 authorization flow

post.description: Deletes the server-side session and clears all auth cookies (access token, refresh token, legacy session)

get.description: Returns information about the currently authenticated user
Returns the list of OAuth providers configured and enabled on this instance

post.description: Validates the refresh token from the httpOnly cookie and issues a new short-lived access token. Rate limited to 30 requests per hour per session

200.description: Access token refreshed, new cookie set

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired refresh token

post.description: Switches the authenticated user's active organization context. Re-issues a new access token (and refresh token) scoped to the chosen org. The user must be a member of the target org

200.description: Switched, new access token set in cookies

403.description: Not a member of the target org

post.description: Creates a Polar Checkout session for the given product/price ID and returns the hosted checkout URL. Optionally accepts a discount code. The caller must be the billing account owner
Generates a Polar Customer Portal URL for the authenticated user. The frontend should redirect the user to the returned URL to manage their subscription, payment methods, and invoices

404.description: No billing account or customer ID found

get.description: Returns the billing account tier, active subscription details (status, period end, cancel-at-period-end), and whether the caller is the billing owner. Auto-creates a billing account for new users if one does not exist

post.description: Receives and processes Polar webhook events (subscription created/updated/cancelled, order created). Verifies the webhook signature before processing. Returns 200 for success or known-skip, 400 for malformed payloads, 503 for transient failures

get.description: Validates an invitation token and returns the organization name and inviter details. Public endpoint — no authentication required. Used by the accept-invite page to show context before the user logs in

404.description: Token not found, expired, or already accepted

post.description: Accepts a pending invitation. Validates the token, verifies the caller's email matches the invited email, adds the user to the organization as the invited role, marks the invitation as accepted, and re-issues a new access token scoped to the new org

200.description: Invitation accepted, new access token set

403.description: Caller email does not match invitation

404.description: Invitation not found or already accepted

409.description: Already a member of this org

get.description: Returns all active API keys for the authenticated user. The raw token is never returned here — only the hint (last 4 chars)

post.description: Generates a new personal access token (PAT) for programmatic API access. The raw token is returned only once — copy it immediately. Requires Pro tier or higher

200.description: API key created with raw token

403.description: Plan does not support API keys

delete.description: Soft-deletes an API key owned by the authenticated user. Returns 204 on success

404.description: Key not found or not owned by user

get.description: Returns a paginated list of links for the authenticated organization

post.description: Creates a new short link for the authenticated organization. Respects monthly tier limits. Optionally accepts a custom short code (Pro+), UTM parameters (Pro+), tags, expiry, and redirect type

403.description: Monthly link limit reached for current tier

get.description: Looks up a link by its short code instead of its internal ID
get.summary: Get a link by short code

get.description: Exports all active links for the authenticated organization as a CSV file. Returns a text/csv response with a Content-Disposition attachment header

post.description: Bulk-imports links from a CSV payload. Accepts a JSON array of rows parsed from CSV. Each row must have at least a destination_url. Returns counts of created, skipped (duplicate short codes), and failed rows

delete.description: Soft-deletes a link belonging to the authenticated organization and removes its KV mapping so it stops redirecting

get.description: Returns details for a single link belonging to the authenticated organization

put.description: Updates a link's destination URL, title, tags, expiry, UTM parameters, redirect type, or forward-query-params setting. Updates are written to both D1 and KV atomically

get.description: Returns click analytics for a single link. The time range is capped according to the organization's tier retention window (7 days for Free, 365 days for Pro, unlimited for Business/Unlimited)

parameters.description: Number of days to look back (default: 7)
parameters.name: days

parameters.description: Unix timestamp range start (alternative to days)
parameters.name: start

get.description: Returns all organizations the authenticated user belongs to, including their role in each org and the org's current billing tier. Also returns the active org_id from the current session

200.description: List of organizations with role and tier info

post.description: Creates a new organization for the authenticated user. The user becomes the owner. Respects tier organization limits

403.description: Org limit reached for current tier

delete.description: Permanently deletes an organization. Requires owner role and the user must belong to at least one other org. Links can either be migrated to another org or deleted

400.description: Cannot delete last org or invalid migration target

get.description: Returns org details including the member list with roles and pending invitations. The caller must be a member of the org

403.description: Not a member of this org

patch.description: Renames an organization. Requires owner or admin role within the org

post.description: Sends an email invitation to join the organization. Requires owner or admin role. Respects tier member limits. Sends an invitation email with a unique token

403.description: Owner or admin required, or member limit reached

409.description: Already a member or invitation pending

delete.description: Cancels a pending invitation. Requires owner or admin role

post.description: Resends the invitation email for a pending invitation. Requires owner or admin role

404.description: Invitation not found or already accepted

delete.description: Removes the organization logo from R2 storage. Requires owner or admin role and Pro+ tier

403.description: Owner or admin required, or Pro+ required

get.description: Serves the organization logo from R2 storage. Public endpoint — no authentication required

404.description: No logo found for this org

post.description: Uploads an organization logo. Accepts multipart/form-data with a field named 'logo'. Max 500 KB. Accepted formats: image/png, image/jpeg, image/webp, image/svg+xml. Requires owner or admin role and Pro+ tier

400.description: Missing file, too large, or unsupported format

403.description: Owner or admin required, or Pro+ required

delete.description: Removes a user from the organization. Owners can remove anyone except the last owner. Admins can remove members but not owners. Any member can remove themselves

get.description: Returns organization-level settings. The query_forwarding_enabled setting is only available on Pro+ tiers

403.description: Not a member of this org

patch.description: Updates organization-level settings. query_forwarding_enabled requires Pro+ tier. Caller must be owner or admin

403.description: Owner or admin required, or Pro+ required

post.description: Submits an abuse report for a link. Accepts both authenticated and anonymous submissions. Duplicate reports for the same link, reason, and reporter within 24 hours are rejected

404.description: Link not found or already removed

get.description: Returns non-sensitive public settings needed by the frontend, including founder pricing status and active discount amounts for each plan tier
Returns all tags used within the authenticated organization, each with a usage count reflecting the number of active links carrying that tag

200.description: Array of tags with usage counts

delete.description: Removes a tag from all links in the authenticated organization and deletes it

patch.description: Renames a tag across all links in the authenticated organization. Returns the updated tag list

400.description: Missing or invalid new tag name

get.description: Returns the authenticated organization's tier, feature limits, current monthly link usage, tag count, and the date/time of the next monthly counter reset

200.description: Usage and limits for the current org

get.description: Returns the running application version, package name, build timestamp, and git commit hash

tags.description: API key management for programmatic access (Pro+ tier)
